page: Amazon Web Services m8gd.metal-24xl is a bare-metal general-purpose server powered by AWS Graviton processors. It features 96 dedicated physical cores built on the 64-bit ARMv9 architecture using the Neoverse-V2 model, running at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz. The processor configuration includes a 36 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 64 KB L1d cache per core. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR5 memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

This server includes 5700 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage. It delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 40 Gbps. The combination of high-capacity local NVMe SSDs and high-speed DDR5 memory supports data-intensive operations without relying solely on network-attached storage.

In benchmark testing, the m8gd.metal-24xl demonstrates top-tier multi-core performance. It achieves top 10% placement in multi-core stress-ng CPU tests, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, and Geekbench multi-core scores. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, scoring in the top 10% for both small-block cached and large-block uncached read and write operations. Database performance is also notable, with Redis SET operations and PassMark database operations reaching top-tier status. Single-core performance is generally strong to average, with Geekbench single-core scores in the top 25% and stress-ng single-core CPU performance in the middle 50%.

While the server excels in multi-core processing, database operations, and static web serving, it exhibits a tradeoff in specific LLM inference tasks. Text generation using a small 135M model falls into the bottom 10%, though prompt processing and text generation for medium and large models remain average to strong. Given its balanced resource density, high-speed local storage, and strong multi-core efficiency, the m8gd.metal-24xl is highly suited for database hosting, high-throughput web serving, software compilation, and multi-threaded data processing workloads.
description: The Amazon Web Services m8gd.metal-24xl is a bare-metal general-purpose server powered by 96 dedicated physical cores of the ARMv9 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 384.0 GB of DDR5 memory, 5700 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and a baseline network bandwidth of 40 Gbps. In benchmark evaluations, the server delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and high-throughput database capabilities, as evidenced by top 10% scores in multi-core stress-ng, Redis SET operations, and PassMark database tests. While single-core performance and small-model LLM text generation show moderate results, the server's high resource density and fast local storage make it highly efficient for multi-threaded workloads. It is well-suited for database hosting, software compilation, static web serving, and parallel data processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8gd.metal-24xl is a bare-metal server featuring 96 ARMv9 Graviton cores, 384 GB DDR5 RAM, and 5700 GB local NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8gd.metal-24xl is a bare-metal server with 96 ARMv9 Graviton cores, 384 GB DDR5 RAM, and 5.7 TB local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A high-density bare-metal platform combining 96 dedicated ARMv9 cores with fast local NVMe storage for demanding multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated ARMv9 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz
384.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory with 4.0 GB per core allocation
5700 GB of integrated local NVMe SSD instance storage
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark performance
Optimized for database hosting, software compilation, and high-throughput web serving
